events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer. Need parse scenario: Fall protection, industrial site, observed field condition: 6-ft trigger ignored. Work rough-complete. Need estimate measurable condition and state why out of tolerance. Reference OSHA 1926 Subpart M (Fall protection in construction) and 1910.140 (general industry). 6-ft trigger: In construction, OSHA 1926.501(b)(1) requires fall protection when working at heights 6 feet or more above lower levels. Ignoring 6-ft trigger means workers exposed to falls of 6+ ft without protection. That is non-compliant, high/critical risk. Decision fail. Work stage rough-complete. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 80? Value maybe 6 (feet trigger). Confidence high. Findings: fall protection not provided at 6 ft, exposed workers to fall hazard. Actions: install guardrails, PFAS, safety nets, training, stop work. Rationale: OSHA requires fall protection at 6 ft in construction; ignoring trigger creates serious hazard. References: OSHA 1926 Subpart M, 1926.501(b)(1), 1910.140 maybe not directly but general. Need output exactly JSON. Ensure no prose outside.

{

  "findings": [
    "Fall protection trigger ignored at the 6-ft exposure threshold.",
    "Workers are exposed to unprotected falls of 6 feet or more to lower levels.",
    "No compliant guardrail, personal fall arrest, safety net, or warning-line system is in place where required."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work in the affected area until compliant fall protection is provided.",
    "Install guardrails, personal fall arrest systems, or safety nets for all work at or above 6 ft.",
    "Verify anchorages, full-body harnesses, lanyards, and connectors meet OSHA and manufacturer requirements.",
    "Brief crew on the 6-ft trigger and site-specific fall-protection plan before resuming work.",
    "Re-inspect and document corrective action before closing out the area."
  ],
  "rationale": "OSHA 1926 Subpart M requires fall protection whenever construction workers are exposed to falls of 6 feet or more. Ignoring the 6-ft trigger means the installed fall-protection scheme is defective and non-compliant, creating a critical fall hazard.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1926 Subpart M",
    "OSHA 1926.501(b)(1)",
    "OSHA 1910.140"
  ]
}
